  "findings": [
    "Calculated tension per sling leg is approximately 2,121.32 lbs, which is well below the tagged vertical WLL of 3,500 lbs."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Proceed with the planned lift using the specified 3,500 lb WLL slings at a 45-degree horizontal angle."
  ],
  "rationale": "The tension in each leg of a symmetric two-leg sling is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(angle). For a 3,000 lb load at a 45-degree horizontal sling angle, the tension per leg is (3,000 / 2) / sin(45°) = 1,500 / 0.7071 = 2,121.32 lbs. Since 2,121.32 lbs is less than the rated capacity of 3,500 lbs, the configuration is safe and compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
